title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: circuitscaper requires Julia (>= 1.9) Circuitscape Omniscape packages. can install everything R: already Julia installed, cs_setup() find automatically can point :
code: # Install circuitscaper (from GitHub during development) # remotes::install_github("matthewkling/circuitscaper")  library(circuitscaper)  # First time only: install Julia and required packages cs_install_julia() cs_setup(julia_home = "/path/to/julia/bin")

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: common Circuitscape analysis computes effective resistance cumulative current flow pairs focal nodes.
code: library(terra) library(circuitscaper)  # Resistance surface resistance <- rast("path/to/resistance.tif")  # Focal nodes (integer IDs) locations <- rast("path/to/focal_nodes.tif")  # Run pairwise analysis result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations)  # Current flow map plot(result$current_map)  # Pairwise resistance matrix result$resistance_matrix

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: one--mode, focal node takes turn source others serve grounds simultaneously.
code: result <- cs_one_to_all(resistance, locations) plot(result$current_map)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: --one reverse: focal nodes sources takes turn ground.
code: result <- cs_all_to_one(resistance, locations) plot(result$current_map)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Advanced mode gives full control source ground placement.
code: source_layer <- rast("path/to/sources.tif") ground_layer <- rast("path/to/grounds.tif")  result <- cs_advanced(resistance, source_layer, ground_layer,                       write_voltage = TRUE)  # Access individual layers plot(result[["cumulative_current"]]) plot(result[["voltage"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: Omniscape computes omnidirectional connectivity using moving window approach.
code: result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 100, block_size = 5)  plot(result[["normalized_current"]]) plot(result[["flow_potential"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
code: source_strength <- rast("path/to/habitat_quality.tif")  result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 100,                  source_strength = source_strength,                  block_size = 5)  plot(result[["normalized_current"]])

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: large landscapes, enable Julia multithreading. Julia’s thread count fixed startup, set via cs_setup() circuitscaper calls:
code: # Set thread count at the start of your session cs_setup(threads = 4)  result <- os_run(resistance, radius = 100,                  block_size = 5,                  parallelize = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: default, intermediate files written temporary directory cleaned . persist output files, use output_dir:
code: result <- cs_pairwise(resistance, locations,                       output_dir = "my_output_directory")

title: Getting Started with circuitscaper
text: CRS preservation: circuitscaper captures CRS input raster reattaches outputs, even though Circuitscape’s ASCII grid format doesn’t carry CRS information. Solver choice: default "cg+amg" solver works well cases. small problems, "cholmod" (direct solver) may faster. Verbose output: Set verbose = TRUE see Circuitscape’s solver progress, useful debugging monitoring long runs.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Matthew Kling. Author, maintainer.

what: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: R interface Circuitscape.jl Omniscape.jl via JuliaCall.

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: circuitscaper provides clean, R-native interface Circuitscape Omniscape landscape connectivity modeling using circuit theory. Users work entirely R familiar terra::SpatRaster objects Julia handles computation invisibly.

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("matthewkling/circuitscaper")  # First time: install Julia and required packages library(circuitscaper) cs_install_julia()

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
code: library(circuitscaper) library(terra)  # Pairwise Circuitscape result <- cs_pairwise(resistance_raster, focal_nodes) plot(result$current_map) result$resistance_matrix  # Omniscape result <- os_run(resistance_raster, radius = 100, block_size = 5) plot(result[["normalized_current"]])

title: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia
text: R >= 4.0 Julia >= 1.9 (installed automatically via cs_install_julia()) R packages: terra, JuliaCall

what: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
title: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
text: Provides R-native interface Circuitscape.jl Omniscape.jl Julia packages landscape connectivity modeling using circuit theory. Users work entirely R familiar objects (SpatRaster, file paths) Julia handles computation invisibly. Supports four Circuitscape modes (pairwise, one--, --one, advanced) Omniscape moving-window analysis.

title: circuitscaper: R Interface to Circuitscape and Omniscape via Julia — circuitscaper-package
text: Maintainer: Matthew Kling mattkling@gmail.com

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Solve single circuit user-specified source ground layers.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
code: cs_advanced(   resistance,   source,   ground,   resistance_is = "resistances",   four_neighbors = FALSE,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. source terra::SpatRaster file path. Source current strengths (amps per cell). Cells value 0 NA sources. ground terra::SpatRaster file path. Ground conductances (siemens per cell). Cells value 0 NA grounds. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory. verbose Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers: cumulative_current Current density cell. voltage Voltage cell. Voltage analogous movement probability decreases distance sources.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Unlike Circuitscape modes, advanced mode iterate focal nodes. Instead, user provides explicit source current ground conductance rasters, single circuit solved. gives full control current injection pattern useful modeling specific scenarios directional movement defined source area destination.

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: Advanced Circuitscape Analysis — cs_advanced
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) src <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) src[1, 1] <- 1 gnd <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) gnd[10, 10] <- 1 result <- cs_advanced(res, src, gnd) plot(result) plot(result[["voltage"]]) } # }

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: focal node turn, inject current focal nodes ground single node.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
code: cs_all_to_one(   resistance,   locations,   resistance_is = "resistances",   four_neighbors = FALSE,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. locations terra::SpatRaster file path. Focal nodes/regions raster integer IDs. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory cleaned automatically. verbose Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers: cumulative_current Current flow summed across iterations. curmap_N Per-node current map focal node N, N integer node ID locations raster. One layer per focal node.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: --one mode iterates focal node. iteration, focal nodes injected 1 amp current , focal node connected ground. produces current map showing current converges node across landscape. mode useful identifying accessible reachable sites network, emphasizing current flow toward ground node. cumulative map sums across iterations highlights cells important connectivity across full set nodes.

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis — cs_all_to_one
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) locs <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) locs[1, 1] <- 1; locs[1, 10] <- 2; locs[10, 5] <- 3 result <- cs_all_to_one(res, locs) plot(result) } # }

what: Install Julia and Required Packages — cs_install_julia

title: One-to-All Circuitscape Analysis — cs_one_to_all
text: One--mode iterates focal node. iteration, focal node injected 1 amp current remaining focal nodes simultaneously connected ground. produces current map showing current spreads node landscape reach others. mode useful mapping well site connected rest focal node network, emphasizing current dispersal source. cumulative map sums across iterations highlights cells important connectivity across full set nodes.

text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) locs <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) locs[1, 1] <- 1; locs[1, 10] <- 2; locs[10, 5] <- 3 result <- cs_one_to_all(res, locs) plot(result) } # }

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. locations terra::SpatRaster file path. Focal nodes/regions raster integer IDs. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". four_neighbors Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory cleaned automatically. verbose Logical. title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: named list : current_map terra::SpatRaster single layer: cumulative_current Current flow summed across pairs, indicating relative importance cell movement corridor. resistance_matrix symmetric numeric matrix pairwise effective resistances focal nodes, node IDs row column names.

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: Pairwise mode iterates every unique pair focal nodes. pair, one node injected 1 amp current connected ground. effective resistance pair recorded, resulting current flow accumulated across pairs cumulative current map highlights important movement corridors. common Circuitscape mode typically used quantify connectivity discrete habitat patches populations. resistance matrix can used distance metric analyses isolation resistance.

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
text: Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

title: Pairwise Circuitscape Analysis — cs_pairwise
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10)) locs <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0) locs[1, 1] <- 1; locs[1, 10] <- 2; locs[10, 5] <- 3 result <- cs_pairwise(res, locs) plot(result$current_map) result$resistance_matrix } # }

what: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Initialize Julia session load Circuitscape Omniscape Julia packages. called automatically first use cs_* os_* function. Call explicitly control Julia path, number threads, pre-warm session.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
code: cs_setup(julia_home = NULL, threads = 1L, quiet = TRUE, ...)

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: julia_home Character. Path Julia installation directory. NULL (default), JuliaCall searches standard locations. threads Integer. Number Julia threads start. Default 1L. Must set Julia initializes — Julia running, thread count changed without restarting R. Set value greater 1 plan use os_run() parallelize = TRUE. quiet Logical. Suppress Julia startup messages. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed JuliaCall::julia_setup().

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Invisibly returns TRUE success.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Following pattern established diffeqr, cs_setup() : Install Julia automatically missing (via installJulia = TRUE). Install Circuitscape Omniscape Julia packages missing. Pass ... JuliaCall::julia_setup() users can set JULIA_HOME, rebuild, etc. Julia initialized, stays warm R session. Subsequent calls cs_setup() return immediately.

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Julia's thread count fixed startup changed mid-session. need parallelization Omniscape, set threads calling circuitscaper function:
code: cs_setup(threads = 4) os_run(resistance, radius = 50, parallelize = TRUE)

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
text: Circuitscape: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/ Omniscape: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Omniscape.jl/latest/

title: Set Up Julia and Load Circuitscape/Omniscape — cs_setup
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cs_setup() cs_setup(threads = 4) cs_setup(julia_home = "/usr/local/julia/bin") } # }

what: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: Performs Omniscape analysis, computing omnidirectional landscape connectivity using moving window approach based circuit theory.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
code: os_run(   resistance,   radius,   source_strength = NULL,   block_size = 1L,   source_threshold = 0,   resistance_is = "resistances",   calc_normalized_current = TRUE,   calc_flow_potential = TRUE,   condition = NULL,   condition_type = NULL,   parallelize = FALSE,   julia_threads = 2L,   solver = "cg+amg",   output_dir = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: resistance terra::SpatRaster file path. resistance (conductance) surface. radius Numeric. Moving window radius pixels. source_strength Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Source strength weights. NULL (default), non-nodata pixels treated sources equal weight. block_size Integer. Aggregation block size source points. Default 1 (aggregation). Increasing significantly speeds computation. source_threshold Numeric. Minimum source strength include pixel. Default 0. resistance_is Character. Whether resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) "conductances". calc_normalized_current Logical. Compute normalized current flow. Default TRUE. calc_flow_potential Logical. Compute flow potential. Default TRUE. condition Optional terra::SpatRaster file path. Conditional layer targeted connectivity analysis. condition_type Character. Determines condition layer used. relevant condition provided. See Omniscape documentation options. parallelize Logical. Use Julia multithreading. Default FALSE. Julia's thread count fixed startup. Julia already initialized without enough threads, warning issued. avoid , call cs_setup() threads argument start session. julia_threads Integer. Number Julia threads parallelize = TRUE. Default 2. Ignored Julia already running fewer threads. solver Character. Solver use: "cg+amg" (default) "cholmod". output_dir Optional character path. provided, output files persist . Default NULL uses temporary directory. verbose Logical. Print Omniscape output. Default FALSE.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: terra::SpatRaster following layers (depending options): cumulative_current Raw cumulative current flow. Always present. Higher values indicate cells carry current across moving-window iterations. flow_potential Expected current homogeneous resistance (calc_flow_potential = TRUE). Reflects spatial configuration sources independently landscape resistance. normalized_current Cumulative current divided flow potential (calc_normalized_current = TRUE). Values greater 1 indicate cells connectivity higher expected given source geometry; values less 1 indicate relative barriers. typically informative layer identifying corridors pinch points.

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
text: Omniscape documentation: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Omniscape.jl/latest/

title: Run Omniscape Moving-Window Connectivity Analysis — os_run
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(terra) res <- rast(nrows = 50, ncols = 50, vals = runif(2500, 1, 10)) result <- os_run(res, radius = 10) plot(result) plot(result[["normalized_current"]]) } # }
